steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Domine: 'Domine' is a noun in the vocative case meaning 'Lord!'

hints.perfect: Perfect 1st person would be 'fui' — 'sum' is present
hints.imperfect: Imperfect 1st person would be 'eram' — 'sum' is present
hints.future: Future 1st person would be 'ero' — 'sum' is present

hints.sumo, sumere, sumpsi, sumptum: These are principal parts of 'sumo' (to take up)
hints.sequor, sequi, secutus sum, —: These are principal parts of 'sequor' (to follow)
hints.scio, scire, scivi, scitum: These are principal parts of 'scio' (to know)

hints.nominative: Nominative is 'Dominus' — 'Domine' with the -e ending is vocative (direct address)
hints.dative: Dative would be 'Domino' — 'Domine' is vocative (O Lord!)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'Domini' ends in -i
